summary: xcms provides AbundanceSimilarityParam for feature grouping based on correlation of feature abundances across samples. xcms provides EicSimilarityParam for feature grouping based on correlation of EICs.
The faahKO dataset consists of samples from 4 mice with knock-out of the fatty acid amide hydrolase and 4 wild type mice.

Abundance correlation analysis can split retention time-based feature groups further. EIC correlation analysis can provide additional sub-grouping of retention time and abundance-based feature groups.
AbundanceSimilarityParam uses a correlation coefficient threshold to group features together.
Any subsequent groupFeature call will sub-group and refine the previously identified feature groups.
